records.title: Legalise a Dutch document for use abroad
records.description: A Dutch document such as a diploma, civil-status record or medical certificate may need legalisation before it can be used abroad. The required route depends on the document type and destination country, including whether an apostille is accepted.

official_source.title: Legalisation of Dutch documents for use abroad

records.ai_summary: Select the document type and destination country before arranging legalisation. Check whether an apostille, Consular Service Centre legalisation, certified copy or sworn translation is required, and use the correct issuing authority.

records.title: Check eligibility for Anw survivor benefit
records.description: A surviving partner may qualify for an Anw survivor benefit when the deceased was insured under the Dutch scheme and the survivor meets the additional statutory conditions. Income and living situation can affect entitlement and the amount.

official_source.title: What are the qualifying conditions for an Anw survivor benefit?

records.ai_summary: Check whether the deceased was insured for Anw and whether the survivor satisfies the age and additional qualifying conditions. Report income and household changes because both can reduce or end the benefit.

records.title: Resolve a rental dispute through the Rent Tribunal
records.description: The independent Rent Tribunal can decide qualifying disputes between residential tenants and landlords about rent levels, maintenance, service charges and certain rent increases. Its scope does not include every type of housing conflict.
records.ai_summary: First identify whether the dispute falls within the Rent Tribunal's residential-housing jurisdiction. Preserve the tenancy agreement, notices, payment records and evidence of maintenance or service-charge issues before starting proceedings.

attributes.dispute_scope: Residential rent, maintenance and service charges

records.title: Understand the Dutch court process
records.description: Dutch court proceedings involve parties with different rights and duties, including suspects, defendants, victims and witnesses. The applicable steps, representation requirements, deadlines and appeal options depend on the type of case.
records.ai_summary: Determine whether negotiation, mediation, a complaints body or court proceedings fit the conflict. If court action is needed, verify the competent court, legal representation rules, costs, evidence and any appeal deadline.

attributes.responsible_authority: Dutch judiciary and Ministry of Justice and Security
attributes.process_scope: Civil, administrative and criminal justice information

records.title: Prepare a household emergency kit
records.description: A household emergency kit should contain supplies needed during a disruption or disaster, including water, shelf-stable food, lighting, power, radio, first aid, hygiene items, cash and copies of important information.

official_source.title: What does the emergency kit contain?

records.ai_summary: Assemble the official emergency-kit essentials for everyone in the household and store them accessibly. Periodically check water, food, batteries, medicines, contact details and copies of identity documents for expiry or changes.

records.title: Register and vote as a Dutch voter abroad
records.description: Dutch nationals living outside the Netherlands must register with the municipality of The Hague to vote from abroad. Depending on the election and circumstances, voting may be possible by post, proxy or at a polling station in the Netherlands.
records.ai_summary: Register with The Hague as a Dutch voter abroad and keep personal details and identity documents current. Before each election, check eligible elections, document deadlines and whether postal, proxy or in-person voting is available.

attributes.voting_methods: Post, proxy or polling station when available

records.title: Use the statutory guarantee for a defective product
records.description: Consumers have a statutory right to a product that performs as reasonably expected. If a normally used purchase fails too soon, the seller may have to repair or replace it free of charge or provide a refund when an adequate solution is not delivered.
records.ai_summary: Contact the seller promptly, explain the defect and request a free repair or replacement under the statutory guarantee. Keep the purchase evidence and communication; the seller remains responsible even when a manufacturer warranty has expired.

records.title: File an inheritance tax return
records.description: The value of an inheritance and the applicable exemption determine whether an heir must file and pay Dutch inheritance tax. The Tax Administration handles the return, and the official deadline is stated in the applicable rules or return letter.
records.ai_summary: Check the inheritance value, relationship-based exemption and return deadline before filing. Gather the deceased person's details, will, asset and debt values, information about all heirs and the Tax Administration return letter.
